title: Atlas Analytics
subtitle: Executive intelligence dashboard for growth teams.
description: Atlas Analytics centralizes product, sales, and marketing metrics in one focused command center so growth teams can review performance, spot movement faster, and share insight without switching between multiple tools.
goal: Help growth teams understand performance quickly and act confidently from one clear analytics experience.
solution: A modular analytics workspace with stronger hierarchy, reusable chart patterns, and clearer task flows across reporting, segmentation, and weekly review workflows.

challenges.title: Scattered reporting
challenges.text: Teams were switching between tools to answer simple questions, which slowed down decisions and made weekly reviews inconsistent.

challenges.title: Weak hierarchy
challenges.text: The original dashboard treated every metric with the same visual weight, so users struggled to identify the most important movement first.

challenges.title: Low confidence
challenges.text: Data states, empty states, and drill-down patterns were inconsistent, reducing trust in the product and making the experience feel unfinished.

discovery: Discovery focused on stakeholder interviews, workflow reviews, analytics dashboard audits, and mapping how growth teams currently collect and present performance insight.

structure.intro: The structure phase simplified information architecture, grouped related reporting blocks, and created a clearer dashboard hierarchy for fast scanning.

design.intro: The design phase translated the structure into a reusable analytics UI system with clearer data states, stronger emphasis, and cleaner comparison patterns.

delivery.intro: Delivery focused on a polished prototype, responsive behavior guidance, and a handoff package that made engineering integration easier and more predictable.
bullets: Clarified the entry point into the reporting journey.
Connected overview charts to deeper drill-down states.
Prepared a reusable component language for engineering handoff.
Documented responsive behavior for smaller breakpoints.

process.integration: Integration notes aligned component behavior, responsive priorities, interaction states, and chart data patterns so development could scale the design system cleanly.

steps.title: Discover
steps.description: Mapped goals, reviewed workflow gaps, and aligned scope with product and growth stakeholders.

steps.title: Structure
steps.description: Simplified the dashboard architecture and grouped information by decision-making priority.

steps.title: Design
steps.description: Built high-fidelity screens and reusable analytics components with clearer hierarchy and data emphasis.

steps.title: Delivery
steps.description: Prepared prototype flows, responsive notes, and implementation-ready assets for handoff.

next_steps: Run another round of usability testing on updated dashboard flows.
Expand chart component states for filtering, empty data, and localization.
Add product analytics hooks to measure reporting completion and engagement.

user_research.method: Research combined stakeholder interviews, competitor benchmarking, task-based reviews of the old flow, and journey mapping around how teams review weekly performance.

persona.name: Maya Okafor
persona.quote: "I need a dashboard that helps me spot movement fast without opening five different tools."
persona.bio: Maya works with product and marketing teams to monitor acquisition, activation, and conversion performance. She needs clarity at a glance and confidence when presenting numbers to leadership.
goals: Find the next action without extra digging.
frustrations: Too many tools for one reporting cycle.
Important metrics do not stand out clearly.

journey_mapping.goal: Review campaign and product performance quickly, then share confident insight with the wider team.

findings.title: Users needed stronger visual guidance
findings.text: The most important metrics and changes were not obvious enough, which slowed down dashboard scanning.

findings.title: Simpler structure reduced hesitation
findings.text: Grouping reports by decision-making priority made it easier for users to move from summary to action.

findings.title: Consistency increased trust
findings.text: Clearer states, reusable patterns, and more coherent drill-down behavior made the dashboard feel more reliable.

outcomes.intro: The final direction created a clearer, faster, and more scalable analytics experience for product and growth teams.
outcomes.impact: Usability feedback showed stronger confidence, easier scanning, and faster completion of key reporting tasks in prototype reviews.
outcomes.learned: The project reinforced the value of combining research clarity, visual hierarchy, and engineering-ready structure in one connected design workflow.
